message:
fix(runtime): unbreak tracing-integration feature by using real param names

The cleanup helpers on `DeferredCleanupQueue`, the `*EpochGC` direct-cleanup
methods, and several `EpochConsistencyTracker` internals declared their
parameters with a `_` prefix (e.g. `_waker_id`, `_region_id`, `_timer_id`,
`_correlation_id`). Their bodies, however, reference the un-prefixed names
inside `tracing::{debug,trace,error}!` / `debug!` calls that are gated on
`#[cfg(feature = "tracing-integration")]`. With the feature off the `_`
silences unused-variable warnings; with the feature on the macros fail to
compile because the identifiers they need don't exist.

Renaming back to the canonical names (`waker_id`, `region_id`, `timer_id`,
`channel_id`, `obligation_id`, `metadata`, `data`, `correlation_id`,
`sync_status`, `processing_latency`, `violation_id`, destructured fields on
`EpochConsistencyViolation::{ModuleDesync,SlowTransition,MissingTransition}`,
`affected_modules`, `epoch_skew`, and the `task_id` loop binding in
`cleanup_region` / `direct_cleanup_region`) lets the feature-gated tracing
actually compile while keeping behaviour identical when the feature is
disabled.

Touched:
- src/runtime/epoch_gc.rs
- src/runtime/epoch_gc_integration.rs
- src/runtime/epoch_tracker.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
